title: rapanea ciliata
intro: Rapanea ciliata, also known as the New Zealand mountain pepper tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Myrsinaceae. It has been used for centuries in traditional medicine, particularly in New Zealand and Australia, due to its medicinal properties, which include antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Rapanea ciliata include its potential to reduce pain and inflammation, as well as its ability to treat respiratory issues and skin conditions. Herbal preparations of Rapanea ciliata can be made by infusing the leaves in hot water or oil, and it is also used as a spice in some traditional dishes.
